extra.message: User input from request.form.get('username') and request.form.get('password') is interpolated directly into an SQL query string via f-string on line 33. This allows an attacker to craft input that modifies the SQL query logic, such as ' OR '1'='1, bypassing authentication entirely or exfiltrating data.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied input from request.args.get('q') is rendered directly into the HTML response on line 53 via f-string without any encoding or sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by supplying a query parameter like ?q=<script>alert('xss')</script>, resulting in reflected cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The 'session' cookie value (which an attacker can arbitrarily set) is reflected unsanitized into the HTML response on line 47 via f-string interpolation. An attacker who sets a cookie like session=<script>malicious_code</script> achieves XSS when a victim visits /dashboard.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The /dashboard route on line 43 has no authentication check whatsoever. It does not validate the session cookie against the database or verify any token. Any user — authenticated or not — can access the dashboard by simply visiting the URL directly. The cookie is only used to read a display name, not to enforce access control.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The session cookie is set on line 38 with no HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite attributes, and stores the plain username. Without HttpOnly, JavaScript can access the cookie, enabling XSS-based session theft. Without Secure, the cookie is sent over unencrypted HTTP. Storing plain usernames exposes internal user identifiers.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: Credentials are hardcoded as global variables on lines 7-8: USERNAME='admin' and PASSWORD='password123'. Hardcoded credentials in source code are discoverable via source code review, version control history, or disk access, and cannot be rotated without code changes.

The application runs on port 80 (HTTP) bound to all interfaces (0.0.0.0) on line 57 without HTTPS or TLS. All traffic — including login credentials and session cookies — is transmitted in cleartext, enabling network-level interception and man-in-the-middle attacks.

The application is bound to 0.0.0.0 on line 57, meaning it listens on all network interfaces. This exposes the service to the entire network, which in combination with running as root (port 80 < 1024 on Linux) and lacking authentication on /dashboard, widens the attack surface unnecessarily.
